description_long: This object is a rectangular, box-shaped radio receiver with a dark brown body and a prominent, angled metallic antenna extending from the top. The front face features a checkered speaker grille on the left, composed of alternating light and dark gray squares, and a set of controls on the right, including a dark rectangular display and a smaller square button or knob below it. The top of the radio has a raised section where the antenna is mounted, giving it a classic portable radio appearance. The sides and back are plain, with the back and left side being solid brown and unadorned. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, reminiscent of mid-20th-century portable radios. The materials suggest a combination of plastic for the casing, metal for the antenna, and internal components likely including electronic circuitry and air gaps.
description: A boxy, dark brown radio receiver with a checkered speaker grille, a metallic antenna, and simple control features on the front. The design is classic and utilitarian, with a raised section for the antenna.
description_view_0: The left side and top of the radio are visible, showing the plain brown casing and the metallic antenna emerging from a raised section.
description_view_1: The back and right side are visible, with the checkered speaker grille partially visible on the front edge and the antenna extending upward.
description_view_2: The front view displays the checkered speaker grille, a rectangular display, a small square button or knob, and the angled antenna on top.
description_view_3: The back and left side are visible, showing the plain brown casing and the base of the antenna.
